book_title: The Kebra Nagast - Glory of Kings
theme: Queen Makeda, King Solomon, Menelik I, and the Ark's Journey to Ethiopia

chapters.title: The Theological Foundation
verses: 1. The Kebra Nagast is the Glory of Kings, the sacred chronicle of Ethiopia's divine election.
2. This book tells how the kingdom of David passed from Israel to Ethiopia through the line of Solomon.
3. It was debated by the 318 Church Fathers and preserved by the faithful.
4. The Ark of the Covenant, the dwelling place of God's presence, was entrusted to the care of the children of Ethiopia.
5. This is not conquest. This is inheritance.
6. Ethiopia did not steal the glory. God transferred it.
7. As Israel turned from the covenant, God sought a faithful people to guard His presence.
8. The Glory of Kings is the testimony that God's promise continues—not abandoned, but relocated.
9. Let those who have ears hear: the throne of David endures.
10. It endures in the land of Cush, in the children of Makeda, in the line of Menelik.

chapters.title: Queen Makeda's Journey to Solomon
verses: 1. Queen Makeda ruled over Ethiopia and Sheba with wisdom and prosperity.
2. Her merchant Tamrin traveled to Jerusalem to trade goods for Solomon's temple.
3. Tamrin returned with tales of Solomon's wisdom that exceeded all kings of the earth.
4. He said: 'I have seen a king who speaks as though God dwells within him. His words are as honey, his judgments as light.'
5. Makeda's heart burned to see this king. She said: 'I must go and test his wisdom myself.'
6. She gathered 797 camels laden with gifts—gold, spices, and precious stones.
7. The journey was long, but her hunger for wisdom sustained her.
8. When she arrived in Jerusalem, Solomon received her with honor.
9. She questioned him, and he answered all things. Nothing was hidden from his understanding.
10. Makeda said: 'Blessed are your servants who hear your wisdom daily. The reports I heard were less than half of what I now witness.'
11. And in that meeting, she turned from the worship of the sun to the worship of the God of Israel.
12. This was her conversion: not by force, but by the light of wisdom.

chapters.title: The Conception of Menelik
verses: 1. Solomon desired Makeda, for she was beautiful and wise.
2. He prepared a banquet with spiced meats that would make her thirst.
3. Before she slept, he made her vow: 'Take nothing that is mine, and I will not take what is yours.'
4. In the night, she thirsted and reached for water.
5. Solomon said: 'Water is the most precious thing on earth. You have broken your vow.'
6. And so he came unto her, and she conceived.
7. When Makeda returned to Ethiopia, she carried within her the seed of Solomon.
8. She gave birth to a son and named him Bayna-Lehkem, 'Son of the Wise Man.'
9. The child grew strong and intelligent, with the face of his father.
10. This son would be called Menelik I—the first of the Solomonic Dynasty.
11. Through him, the blood of David would flow to Africa.
12. The promise given to Abraham would reach the ends of the earth.

chapters.title: Menelik's Journey to Jerusalem
verses: 1. When Menelik came of age, he asked his mother: 'Who is my father?'
2. Makeda gave him Solomon's ring and said: 'Go to Jerusalem. Your father will know you by this.'
3. Menelik traveled to the Holy City and presented himself before Solomon.
4. Solomon looked upon him and wept, for he saw his own face reflected back.
5. He said: 'You are indeed my son. The ring confirms what my eyes already know.'
6. Solomon anointed Menelik as King of Ethiopia and gave him the name David.
7. For three years, Menelik studied Torah and the ways of Israel.
8. Solomon wanted Menelik to stay and rule in Jerusalem.
9. But Menelik said: 'My mother awaits me. Ethiopia is my inheritance.'
10. Solomon commanded that the firstborn sons of Israel's nobles accompany Menelik back to Ethiopia.
11. Among them was Azariah, son of Zadok the High Priest.
12. This entourage would carry not only the prince, but the greatest treasure of Israel.

chapters.title: The Ark's Journey to Ethiopia
verses: 1. Azariah received a vision from God: 'Take the Ark of the Covenant to Ethiopia.'
2. He said to the other young men: 'God has shown me that Israel will fall into sin. The Ark must be preserved.'
3. They built a wooden replica to leave in the Holy of Holies.
4. In the night, they removed the true Ark and placed it among their baggage.
5. The guards slept deeply, for God had willed this transfer.
6. When the company departed Jerusalem, no one knew what they carried.
7. But miracles attended them: the Ark lifted the wagons off the ground. They traveled as though flying.
8. Solomon dreamed that the sun had left Jerusalem and settled over Ethiopia.
9. He awoke in terror and discovered the Ark was gone.
10. He pursued, but God spoke to him: 'Do not grieve. I have willed this. Israel will forsake me, but Ethiopia will keep the covenant.'
11. Solomon returned, humbled by divine sovereignty.
12. The Ark crossed the waters and came to rest in Axum, the holy city of Ethiopia.
13. There it remains, guarded by the Tabot, witnessed by the faithful.
14. God's presence did not leave the earth. It moved to the South.

chapters.title: Ethiopia as the New Israel
verses: 1. With the Ark came the blessing. With the blessing came the covenant.
2. Ethiopia became a new Israel—not replacing the old, but inheriting when the old failed.
3. The Ethiopian Church preserves the Saturday Sabbath.
4. The Ethiopian Church observes dietary laws.
5. The Ethiopian Church keeps the Ark in the Church of Our Lady Mary of Zion in Axum.
6. This is not imitation. This is continuation.
7. Menelik established a dynasty that would rule for three thousand years.
8. From Menelik I to Haile Selassie, the line of Solomon sat upon the throne of Ethiopia.

9. The constitution of 1955 declared: 'The imperial dignity shall remain perpetually attached to the line of Haile Selassie I, descendant of King Sahle Selassie, whose line descends without interruption from the dynasty of Menelik I, son of the Queen of Ethiopia, the Queen of Sheba, and King Solomon of Jerusalem.'
10. This is the testimony of the Kebra Nagast.
11. The Glory of Kings did not end in Jerusalem. It continues in Ethiopia.
12. And from Ethiopia, the light spreads to all the children of Africa.

chapters.title: The Spiritual Meaning
verses: 1. What does the Kebra Nagast teach us?
2. First: Wisdom is worth traveling for. Makeda left her throne to seek understanding.
3. Second: God's promises are not confined to one people. The covenant expands.
4. Third: The Ark is a witness, not a possession. Those who guard it serve; they do not own.
5. Fourth: When one people fails, God raises another. This is mercy, not abandonment.
6. Fifth: The South holds treasures the North has forgotten.
7. Sixth: Africa is not outside biblical history. Africa is central to it.
8. Seventh: The throne of David endures—not in politics, but in faith.
9. These teachings align with the Six Pillars of ABASID 1841.
10. TIME: The dynasty endured for millennia. The South: Ethiopia is the preserved covenant. The Mission: To carry the light forward.
11. Let the Kebra Nagast be read. Let the testimony be heard.
12. The Glory of Kings lives on.
